summary: Strong DevOps maturity with comprehensive CI/CD pipeline, reproducible builds via Cargo.lock, and containerized testing. Modern GitHub Actions setup includes build, test, format, lint, and E2E stages with caching. Dockerfile uses multi-stage-like approach with proper layer caching. Missing: secret management patterns, branch protection documentation, explicit deployment strategy.

build_reproducibility.notes: Cargo.lock ensures reproducible builds. Uses dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable (latest stable) rather than pinned MSRV. Good for most projects; could pin MSRV for stronger reproducibility.

containerization.notes: Uses official rust:latest base image with proper layer caching strategy (copy Cargo.toml first, then build). Single-stage by design. Missing: non-root user, .dockerignore file, explicit version pin on base image.

secret_management.notes: No explicit secret management observed. Project is editor-source, not a service, so limited secret needs. Should document this for future integrations.

deployment.notes: Not applicable - Alfred is a local editor binary, not a deployed service. Distributed via cargo install.

iac.notes: Not applicable - no infrastructure deployment. Development/CI defined in GitHub Actions and Makefile.

branch_protection.notes: No branch protection rules configured on GitHub. All CI checks pass but not required before merge. Recommend protecting main with required CI checks for a published project.

recommendations.title: Enable branch protection on main
recommendations.description: Require all CI checks (check, test, format, lint, e2e) to pass before merge. Optionally require reviews. This prevents accidental regressions.

recommendations.title: Pin Rust toolchain MSRV in CI
recommendations.description: Replace dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable with explicit MSRV (e.g., 1.70). Document MSRV in Cargo.toml [package] section for crates that support it. Improves reproducibility.

recommendations.title: Add .dockerignore and improve E2E Dockerfile
recommendations.description: Create .dockerignore to exclude unnecessary files (target/, .git/, etc.). Consider multi-stage build: builder stage compiles binary, runtime stage is smaller. Add non-root user.

recommendations.title: Add build matrix for multiple Rust versions
recommendations.description: Test against MSRV and latest stable in CI to catch compatibility issues early. Adds minimal overhead with GitHub Actions parallelism.

recommendations.title: Document deployment and versioning strategy
recommendations.description: Create docs/RELEASE.md explaining how releases are cut, versioned (semver?), and distributed. Add GitHub release workflow if publishing to crates.io.
